description: Census Northern Ireland Household Schema

contents.description: Circumstances may have changed during the coronavirus pandemic. Answer all questions based on the situation as it is now.

description.text: In this section, we are counting the people at {household_address} on Sunday {census_date}.

list: names of people living at this address, including anyone currently away

list.text: names of visitors staying overnight in this household on {census_date}

contents.title: You will need to know

contents.description: The Householder is responsible for ensuring that this questionnaire is completed and submitted.

question.title: What is your name?

options.label: No, I don’t usually live here
options.value: No, I don’t usually live here

contents.description: This is often your permanent or family home.

description.text: If you have more than one address, include yourself at the home address where you spend most of your time. If you split your time equally then use the home address where you are staying overnight on {census_date}.

contents.description: Students, include yourself at both your term-time and out of term-time addresses.
Armed forces members, include yourself at your home address if you have one.

title.text: Do you usually live at {household_address}?

answers.label: Select all that apply. You can select more than one for each person

description.text: Include babies born on or before {census_date}, children, students and schoolchildren who live away from home during term time

options.label: People who usually live outside the UK who are staying in the UK for 3 months or more
options.value: People who usually live outside the UK who are staying in the UK for 3 months or more

options.description: For example, working away, on holiday, in the armed forces, living in an establishment such as a care home for up to 6 months, abroad for up to a year
options.label: People temporarily away

options.description: For example, UK residents between addresses or currently without a home
options.label: People staying temporarily who usually live in the UK but do not have another UK address
options.value: People staying temporarily who usually live in the UK but do not have another UK address

options.label: None of these apply, I am the only person who usually lives here
options.value: None of these apply, I am the only person who usually lives here

contents.description: This is often their permanent or family home.

description.text: If someone has more than one home address, include them at this address if they generally spend most of their time here. For example, children whose parents live apart. If they split their time equally then include them at this address if they are staying overnight on {census_date}.

contents.description: Remember to include

list: students, if this is either their term-time or their out of term-time address
people with a second address for work, if this is their home address
armed forces members, if this is their home address

contents.description: We ask this question to help ensure that everyone is correctly counted in the census. This includes people who are staying temporarily or are away.
The information is vital for planning services, whether it is for hospital beds, school places, GP and dental services or where to build houses and roads.

options.label: Yes, I need to add someone
options.value: Yes, I need to add someone

forms.one: No, there is {cardinality} person living here
forms.other: No, there are {cardinality} people living here

options.value: No, I do not need to add anyone

list: students and schoolchildren who live away from home during term time
people who work away from home within the UK if this is their permanent or family home
members of the armed forces if this is their permanent or family home
people who are temporarily outside the UK for less than 12 months
people staying, or expecting to stay, in an establishment such as a hospital, care home or hostel for less than 6 months
other people who usually live here but are temporarily away from home
people staying temporarily who usually live in the UK but do not have another UK address for example, relatives, friends
people who usually live outside the UK who are staying in the UK for 3 months or more

contents.description: In this section, we’ll ask you how the people who usually live in this household are related to each other.
For the displayed household members, select the appropriate relationship from the options shown. The selected relationship will display at the bottom of the page for you to check.

contents.description: To get a true picture of the working population, we ask this question of everyone who is not currently working. We ask people who are retired because the number of people continuing to work after retirement is increasing. We ask people who are not working due to long-term illness or disability because some intend to go back to work.
